headline: Meta faces Tennessee trial over Instagram allegations
summary:
STORY: Tennessee state claims Instagram's design is to blame for a youth mental-health crisis.

Meta Platforms goes to court on Monday – one of several trials in the coming weeks...

testing allegations that the company's social media platforms were intentionally built to be addictive.

Tennessee accuses the company of violating the state's consumer protection law...

by knowingly designing a product that drives teens to compulsive use...

and misleading the public about its safety.

The lawsuit, filed by the attorney general's office, claims Meta failed to disclose extensive internal research..

showing Instagram could harm teens.

And that it continued offering features it knew were dangerous without warning users.

A Meta spokesperson said in a statement on Friday that the company already has built-in controls...

to protect the hundreds of thousands of Tennessee teens who use social media every day.

The company has argued the state's claims are based on the content posted on Instagram by its users...

and that a federal law shields the company from liability for third-party content.

The trial is slated to last for seven weeks.

It's scheduled to overlap with at least two other trials against the company in courts in California.

Meta faces thousands of lawsuits over similar claims in both state and federal court.

Nearly every state in the country has filed claims against Meta over its platforms’ alleged impact on children.

content.title: Meta faces Tennessee trial over Instagram allegations
content.summary:

Nearly every state in the country has filed claims against Meta over its platforms’ alleged impact on children.

headline: AMD Stock Rises on Vote of Confidence From a Big AI Chip Customer
summary: Microsoft plans to use AMD’s Helios AI infrastructure platform and EPYC data-center processors to power more of its Azure cloud-computing services, the two companies said Monday.  AMD stock jumped 5.1% to $521.23 on Monday after the market open, while the  rose 0.7%.  A combination of Microsoft and AMD technology will power new tools for data processing, electronic design automation, and inference, or running AI models.

headline: Magnificent Seven Dominate S&P 500 Earnings, But That's Changing Dramatically
summary: The Magnificent Seven will again reign supreme over S&P 500 earnings, but their dominance is fading.  As second-quarter earnings reports start to flow in, analysts expect the Magnificent Seven companies to grow earnings by a combined 31.1%.  The other 493 S&P 500 companies are expected to increase earnings 22.8%, FactSet Senior Earnings Analyst John Butters wrote in a report.
